title: A Craft and a Grove
intro: A stretch of Runehold's land falls into dispute: the Brown want it for a stone-quarry, the Green for a sacred grove. Both have sent gifts ahead of their asking, and both will remember which way you rule.

advisors.text: The quarry means good stone and a guild in our debt, Archon. A grove means birdsong. Rule with your head.
A grove is a living thing that gives for a hundred years; a quarry is a hole. The Green will not forget which you chose.

choices.label: Grant the land to the Brown for a quarry

win.text: The Brown break ground with a will, and good stone and Brown goodwill flow to Runehold — while the Green mourn a grove unplanted.
win.saga: You gave the disputed land to the Brown for a quarry, gaining stone and the delta's favour and the Green's cold shoulder.

choices.label: Grant the land to the Green for a grove

win.text: The Green plant a grove that bears within the year, and their friendship blooms with it — while the Brown pack up their tools, unpaid and unpleased.
win.saga: You gave the disputed land to the Green for a grove, gaining harvest and the jungle's favour and the Brown's resentment.

win.text: You draw a line both can live with, and your fairness wins a grudging nod from each. A quarry and a grove, and no enemy made.
win.saga: You split the disputed land fairly between Brown and Green, and your even hand won a grudging warmth from both.

lose.text: Your compromise pleases no one — each side sure the other got the better half, and both a little colder for it.
lose.saga: Your attempt to split the disputed land satisfied neither Brown nor Green, cooling both against Runehold.
